title: Claude Sonnet 4 Pro vs ChatGPT Plus: The Future of Autonomous Coding on GitHub [JUNE 2025]
author: Riccardo Bernardi
text:
Member-only story
Claude Sonnet 4 Pro vs ChatGPT Plus: The Future of Autonomous Coding on GitHub [JUNE 2025]
How Next-Gen AI Assistants Are Transforming Code Automation, PRs, and Documentation for Developers
The rise of AI coding assistants has transformed the way developers write code, manage repositories, and collaborate on projects. But as the landscape matures, the real question isn’t just which model writes better code — it’s which one can truly automate your workflow, from code generation to pull requests and documentation.
If you’re a developer looking to streamline your GitHub workflow, you’ve probably considered the two leading AI assistants: Claude Sonnet 4 Pro and ChatGPT Plus (GPT-4o). Both are powerful, but when it comes to hands-off, autonomous repo management, the differences are dramatic.
Let’s dive into how these tools stack up, especially if your dream is to have an AI not just write code, but also open pull requests, make commits, and keep your documentation up to date.
What Can Claude Sonnet 4 Pro Actually Do?
Here’s how a typical workflow might look with Claude Sonnet 4 Pro:
- You open an issue in your GitHub repository:
“Add a simple website with login for personal finance tracking.”
